decks.notes: This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on utilizing low-cost creatures and efficient removal spells to quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck's key strategy involves playing aggressive creatures like Kor Skyfisher and Glint Hawk to pressure the opponent, while also using cards like Galvanic Blast and Lightning Bolt to remove threats. The inclusion of artifacts like Melded Moxite and Relic of Progenitus helps to maintain card advantage and disrupt the opponent's graveyard strategies. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Journey to Nowhere for additional removal and cards like Dawnbringer Cleric for added utility.

The main idea of the deck is to aggressively play low cost creatures and use burn spells to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. The deck includes cards that can deal direct damage, drain life from the opponent, and gain life for the player. It also features cards that allow for card draw and provide mana fixing. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain a steady stream of damage to overwhelm the opponent.

This Pauper 1 vs 1 aggro deck focuses on Elves, utilizing cards like Llanowar Elves, Priest of Titania, and Quirion Ranger to ramp up mana quickly and overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures like Nyxborn Hydra and Timberwatch Elf. The deck also includes draw spells like Lead the Stampede and Winding Way to maintain card advantage. The goal is to swarm the board with elves, buff them with Timberwatch Elf, and quickly finish off opponents before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main focus of this Mono Blue Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with low-cost, efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent, along with tempo spells like Brainstorm and Counterspell, the deck aims to control the battlefield and push damage through before the opponent can establish their own game plan. The deck also features card draw and graveyard interactions to maintain card advantage and enable powerful plays. Overall, this deck is designed to apply pressure and disrupt the opponent's strategy effectively.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using a combination of card draw, countermagic, and removal to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It seeks to disrupt the opponent's strategy while developing its own board presence with efficient threats like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic. The deck aims to control the game through a mix of reactive and proactive plays, using cards like Thought Scour and Brainstorm to dig for answers and enable powerful plays. Ultimately, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition and resource advantage.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with creature cards and then use cards like Dread Return and Malevolent Rumble to reanimate powerful creatures like Lotleth Giant and Scrapwork Mutt. Cards like Gnaw to the Bone help to keep the player alive while they set up their combo. The deck also includes ways to search for key pieces and mana fixing to ensure a smoother gameplay. Overall, the strategy revolves around utilizing the graveyard as a resource to swarm the board with powerful creatures and overwhelm the opponent.

The primary idea of this Mono Blue Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent, along with card draw and selection spells like Thought Scour and Ponder, the deck aims to maintain board presence and tempo advantage. With a focus on countering spells and disrupting the opponent's strategy, the deck can quickly close out games by attacking relentlessly and controlling the board.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells and card draw, while also utilizing cheap graveyard synergies to efficiently reduce the cost of powerful threats like Cryptic Serpent and Tolarian Terror. Lórien Revealed provides information advantage, while Sneaky Snacker can generate incremental card advantage. Deem Inferior and Sleep of the Dead provide additional control elements, allowing the deck to maintain board dominance and outvalue the opponent. Contaminated Aquifer offers utility as a land, and Abandon Attachments can help deal with problematic permanents.

This Jund aggro deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's resources while steadily applying pressure with a mix of powerful creatures and removal spells. By utilizing cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Nihil Spellbomb to disrupt the opponent's mana base and graveyard, paired with efficient removal such as Cast Down and Snuff Out, the deck aims to control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot. With a versatile suite of threats and answers, this deck looks to overwhelm opponents with a steady stream of damage while stifling their plans.

The main concept of this deck is to control the board using Gates synergy and removal spells, while also generating value through cards like Raffine's Informant and Thraben Charm. The deck aims to stall the game with defensive creatures like Sacred Cat and Basilisk Gate, while setting up for powerful plays with Writhing Chrysalis and Crimson Fleet Commodore. The inclusion of cards like Guardian of the Guildpact and Journey to Nowhere helps to deal with threats efficiently, ultimately allowing the player to outlast their opponent and secure victory through attrition.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the Balustrade Spy combo to quickly mill through your deck and put key cards into your graveyard. This allows you to generate massive amounts of mana, either through creatures like Gatecreeper Vine and Saruli Caretaker, or through spells like Lotus Petal and Land Grant. Once you have enough mana, you can use Dread Return to bring back powerful creatures like Lotleth Giant or Troll of Khazad-dûm to finish off your opponent. The deck also includes cards like Generous Ent and Winding Way to help find your combo pieces quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using a combination of direct burn spells and aggressive creatures. By efficiently managing resources and maximizing damage output, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize or gain control of the game. With a mix of cheap spells and efficient creatures, this deck seeks to maintain pressure and close out the game as quickly as possible through focused aggression and direct damage.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent through aggressive creatures and burn spells. It aims to control the board with efficient removal and use cards like Sneaky Snacker and Guttersnipe to increase damage output. Faithless Looting helps with card selection and provides additional reach with Flashback spells. The deck's strategy is to build a strong board presence early on and finish the game quickly with direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of countermagic, removal spells, and card draw. By disrupting the opponent's game plan and maintaining card advantage, the Dimir Control player aims to outlast their opponent and win through incremental advantage. The deck also utilizes creatures with disruptive abilities, such as Spellstutter Sprite, to further hinder the opponent's strategy. With a mix of efficient removal, counterspells, and card selection, this deck aims to control the pace of the game and ultimately come out on top.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through removal spells like Cast Down and Defile, while also generating card advantage with cards like Ichor Wellspring and Lembas. The deck also utilizes creatures like Crypt Rats and Troll of Khazad-dûm to control the board and has recursion elements with cards like Pulse of Murasa and Reckoner's Bargain. Additionally, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's graveyard strategies with cards like Nihil Spellbomb and Bojuka Bog. Overall, this Golgari/Jund Garden control deck focuses on staying ahead of the opponent in resources and board presence.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells and removal spells to disrupt opponents' strategies while also gaining card advantage through card draw and graveyard interaction. It aims to stabilize the board with threats like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic while maintaining control of the game through counterspells like Counterspell and Spell Pierce. The deck also utilizes cards like Thought Scour and Lórien Revealed to quickly cycle through the deck and find key pieces. Overall, this deck excels at manipulating the game state and outlasting opponents in a grindy matchup.
